title: LSH Version Control: Ternary Semantic Versioning Based on Three-Layer Architecture
description: We present LSH Version Control, a ternary semantic versioning system grounded in the LSH three-layer architecture (Structure·Rule·Execution). The core insight is that version carry rules should mirror architectural layers: each version digit cycles through three states (0→1→2) before carrying to the next digit, implementing a base-3 carry rule. We systematically compare carry bases from 2 to 10 and demonstrate that base-3 is optimal for the execution layer through three criteria: architectural consistency, fractal self-similarity, and discipline enforcement.
